commonProblems.h2: Toyota Mirai Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Toyota Mirai's hydrogen fuel cell system is generally reliable but has documented failure points that drive replacement enquiries. The JPD10/JPD20 fuel cell stacks and DC/DC converters dominate UK repair demand. Every rebuilt fuel cell system supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address original failure points - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use this decision tool to compare repair vs replace costs.

problems.h4: Fuel Cell Stack Degradation - Why Mirai FC Stacks Lose Power Over Time
problems.affectedModels: Toyota Mirai Design, Design Plus, Design Premium Pack (2015-2024, JPD10-FC & JPD20-FC)
problems.rootCause: Membrane electrode assembly wear and catalyst layer degradation reduce proton exchange efficiency, triggering warning lights and power loss.

repairOptions.tier: Diagnostic & Stack Conditioning - Software reset and performance calibration
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Dealer-level diagnostics, stack conditioning cycle, ECU reflash
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary mitigation; does not reverse physical degradation

repairOptions.tier: MEA Module Replacement - Replace membrane electrode assembly only
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Partial stack disassembly, MEA replacement, seal renewal, bench testing
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if stack housing is intact; 20,000-40,000 mile extension

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt FC Stack - Complete refurbished fuel cell stack unit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt stack, upgraded MEA, full system calibration, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for high-mileage vehicles; restores near-new performance

problems.recommendation: On a Mirai valued under £15,000, a full rebuilt FC stack often represents better long-term value than partial repairs. For newer second-gen models under warranty, pursue dealer conditioning first.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt JPD10/JPD20 fuel cell stack prices for your Toyota Mirai

problems.h4: DC/DC Converter Overvoltage Failure - Sudden System Shutdown Risk
problems.affectedModels: Toyota Mirai First Generation (2015-2020, JPD10-FC with DCDC-BOOST-01)
problems.rootCause: Voltage regulator capacitor degradation causes overvoltage protection to trigger, cutting power to the drivetrain without warning.

repairOptions.tier: Converter Diagnostic & Reset - Fault code clear and voltage test
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: OBD diagnostics, voltage profiling, ECU reset
repairOptions.longevity: Only addresses intermittent faults; high recurrence risk

repairOptions.tier: DC/DC Converter Replacement - Swap faulty converter unit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove old converter, install rebuilt unit, system calibration
repairOptions.longevity: Effective fix; 30,000+ mile expected life with quality unit

repairOptions.tier: Full Power Electronics Overhaul - Converter + related components
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace converter, inspect wiring harness, update control software
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage first-gen Mirai; prevents cascade failures

problems.recommendation: The DC/DC converter is a known first-gen weakness. If your Mirai has experienced sudden shutdowns, budget for converter replacement before strandings occur. Second-gen models have improved designs.
problems.cta: Find rebuilt DC/DC converters for Toyota Mirai First Generation

problems.h4: Hydrogen Tank Seal Leakage - Safety-Critical Component Failure
problems.affectedModels: Toyota Mirai All Variants (2015-2024, H2-TANK-700 System)
problems.rootCause: Tank seal degradation or impact damage allows hydrogen seepage, triggering odour warnings and mandatory safety shutdowns.

repairOptions.tier: Leak Diagnostic & Seal Inspection - Pressure test and visual check
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Hydrogen leak detection, seal inspection, pressure certification
repairOptions.longevity: Identifies issue; does not repair confirmed leaks

repairOptions.tier: Seal Renewal Only - Replace tank seals and fittings
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Tank removal, seal replacement, pressure re-certification, safety testing
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if tank structure is undamaged; requires certified technician

repairOptions.tier: Full Tank Replacement - New or rebuilt Type IV tank unit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of certified tank, full system pressure test, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for impact-damaged tanks or high-mileage vehicles

problems.recommendation: Hydrogen tank work requires FCEV-certified technicians. Never attempt DIY repairs. If a leak is confirmed, replacement with a certified unit is the only safe, warranty-compliant solution.
problems.cta: Get quotes for certified Toyota Mirai hydrogen tank replacement

problems.h4: Power Converter & Injector Failures - Performance Loss Components
problems.affectedModels: Toyota Mirai All Variants (2015-2024, PCU-INV & Injector Systems)
problems.rootCause: Power converter capacitor wear or hydrogen injector sticking reduces system efficiency, causing power loss and warning lights.

repairOptions.tier: Component Diagnostic - Fault code read and injector test
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: OBD scan, injector flow test, converter voltage profiling
repairOptions.longevity: Identifies faulty component; does not repair

repairOptions.tier: Single Component Replacement - Replace faulty injector or converter
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove faulty part, install rebuilt unit, system calibration
repairOptions.longevity: Cost-effective if only one component has failed

repairOptions.tier: Power System Overhaul - Multiple component replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace converter, injectors, related sensors, full calibration
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage vehicles with multiple warnings

problems.recommendation: Power electronics failures often cascade. If one component fails on a high-mileage Mirai, consider inspecting related parts to avoid repeat repairs.
problems.cta: Compare Toyota Mirai power converter and injector replacement prices

faq.h2: Toyota Mirai Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Toyota Mirai fuel cell stack?
items.answer: Replacing a Toyota Mirai fuel cell stack typically costs £4,000-£15,000 depending on generation and whether you choose rebuilt, reconditioned or used components. First-generation JPD10 stacks are generally less expensive than second-generation JPD20 units.
keyPoints: First-gen JPD10 rebuilt stacks: £4,000-£12,000 supply only
Second-gen JPD20 rebuilt stacks: £6,000-£15,000 supply only
Supply & fit labour adds £800-£2,500 depending on specialist
Warranty coverage may reduce or eliminate owner costs
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Toyota Mirai fuel cell stack prices

items.question: Does the Toyota Mirai have a traditional engine?
items.answer: No, the Toyota Mirai uses a hydrogen fuel cell electric powertrain, not a conventional internal combustion engine. It generates electricity from hydrogen and oxygen to power an electric motor, emitting only water vapour.
keyPoints: Fuel cell stack replaces traditional engine block
134kW output equivalent to 182hp conventional engine
No engine oil, spark plugs or exhaust system required
Requires hydrogen refuelling infrastructure, not petrol/diesel
items.cta: Learn about Toyota Mirai FCEV powertrain technology

items.question: Is it worth replacing the fuel cell on an older Toyota Mirai?
items.answer: It depends on your Mirai's value, mileage and warranty status. On a first-gen Mirai valued under £15,000, a £5,000-£10,000 FC stack replacement may not be economical unless covered by warranty or extended coverage.
keyPoints: First-gen Mirai values: £8,000-£20,000 depending on condition
Second-gen Mirai values: £25,000-£45,000 typically
Check Toyota warranty and any extended FCEV coverage first
Consider selling as-is vs investing in major FCEV repairs
items.cta: Get a valuation-based quote for your Toyota Mirai repair decision

items.question: How long does a Toyota Mirai fuel cell stack last?
items.answer: Toyota designs Mirai fuel cell stacks for 150,000+ miles, but real-world degradation warnings often appear around 50,000 km (31,000 miles) or 3 years, especially on first-generation models.
keyPoints: Manufacturer target lifespan: 150,000+ miles
Common warning light trigger: ~50,000 km / 3 years
Second-generation JPD20 stacks show improved durability
Regular hydrogen system maintenance extends component life
items.cta: Check Toyota Mirai fuel cell stack lifespan data

items.question: What causes Toyota Mirai DC/DC converter failure?
items.answer: DC/DC converter overvoltage failures on first-generation Mirai are typically caused by capacitor degradation and thermal stress, triggering sudden system shutdowns as a safety precaution.
keyPoints: Capacitor wear reduces voltage regulation capability
Thermal cycling accelerates component degradation
Software updates can mitigate but not prevent hardware failure
Rebuilt converters with upgraded capacitors offer longer life
items.cta: Find rebuilt DC/DC converters for Toyota Mirai

items.question: Is the Toyota Mirai hydrogen tank safe after replacement?
items.answer: Yes, when replaced by an FCEV-certified specialist using genuine or certified rebuilt Type IV tanks with full pressure certification and documentation, hydrogen tank safety is maintained to original standards.
keyPoints: Type IV tanks use carbon-fibre composite with polymer liner
Replacement requires certified technician and pressure testing
Full documentation provided for warranty and MOT compliance
Never attempt DIY hydrogen system repairs
items.cta: Get quotes for certified Toyota Mirai hydrogen tank replacement

items.question: Does Toyota warranty cover Mirai fuel cell stack replacement?
items.answer: Toyota's standard warranty covers the fuel cell stack for 3 years/60,000 miles in the UK; extended FCEV component coverage may apply - check your specific vehicle's warranty documentation.
keyPoints: Standard warranty: 3 years / 60,000 miles on FC stack
Some models may have extended FCEV component coverage
Warranty claims require Toyota dealer or approved FCEV specialist
Keep full service history to support warranty claims
items.cta: Verify your Toyota Mirai warranty coverage status

items.question: Can any garage replace a Toyota Mirai hydrogen system?
items.answer: No, hydrogen system work on the Mirai requires FCEV-certified technicians with specialised training, equipment and safety protocols. Only use Toyota dealers or approved FCEV specialists.
keyPoints: Hydrogen handling requires specific safety certification
FC stack calibration needs Toyota-level diagnostic tools
Improper installation can void warranty and create safety risks
EngineMarket connects you only with vetted FCEV specialists
items.cta: Find FCEV-certified Toyota Mirai specialists near you

items.question: How long does it take to replace a Mirai fuel cell stack?
items.answer: A complete Toyota Mirai fuel cell stack replacement typically takes 1-3 working days depending on specialist workload, parts availability and whether additional hydrogen system components require attention.
keyPoints: Standard replacement time: 1-3 working days
Parts availability can affect turnaround, especially for Gen 2
Post-installation calibration and testing adds several hours
Supply & fit quotes include estimated completion time
items.cta: Get a timeline quote for your Toyota Mirai FC replacement

items.question: What are the symptoms of Toyota Mirai fuel cell failure?
items.answer: Common symptoms include the "Check Fuel Cell System" warning light, reduced power output, decreased driving range, unusual noises from the FC stack area, or sudden system shutdowns while driving.
items.cta: Diagnose your Toyota Mirai fuel cell warning symptoms
